description_long: This is a large, round, and deep ceramic bowl with a wide open mouth and a slightly irregular rim. The surface is matte and unglazed, showing a natural, earthy gray-brown color with visible cracks and repairs, indicating age and previous breakage. The bowl's body is thick and robust, with a rough texture and no visible handles or decorative elements. Several fracture lines and missing sections are apparent, especially along the rim and one side, suggesting it has been reconstructed from multiple pieces. The interior is similarly rough and unadorned, matching the exterior in color and finish. Small labels or tags are affixed to the surface, likely for cataloging or identification purposes.
description: A large, round, gray-brown ceramic bowl with a rough, matte surface and visible cracks and repairs. The bowl is thick-walled, deep, and shows signs of age and damage.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's wide opening, with prominent cracks and a missing section on the rim, as well as a catalog label on the exterior.
description_view_1: The bowl is seen from the opposite side, highlighting the smooth curve of the body and the extent of the rim's damage and repairs.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the bowl's depth and the continuity of the fracture lines, with the base partially visible.
description_view_3: The bowl's interior is more visible here, showing the rough, unglazed surface and the irregular rim with missing pieces.
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, or as a historical artifact

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
